rulings.Case Title: BORIS JAMES DAVISON VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Renewed Mtn For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 14, 2011, Line 3. PLAINTIFF BORIS DAVISON Renewed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement. Denied. Based on the evidence presented, the court cannot determine that the proposed settlement is fair, adequate and reasonable. The release is overbroad and there are serious concerns about the terms of the settlement and whether plaintiff¿s counsel can adequately represent the settlement class. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARK TANNER CONSTRUCTION, INC. et al VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Quash Servcie Of Summons And Dismiss Complaint For Lack Of Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 14, 2011, Line 5. DEFENDANT TWIN BRIDGES (BERMUDA), LTD. Motion To Quash Servcie Of Summons And Dismiss Complaint For Lack Of Jurisdiction. Granted. Plaintiff fails to present competent evidence demonstrating that Twin Bridges has sufficient contacts with California to warrant the exercise of either general jurisdiction or specific jurisdiction in this case. The fact that the court determined that Twin Bridges is subject to specific jurisdiction in another case does not, in and of itself, show that specific jurisdiction is warranted in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHESTER CHEUNG VS. DARREN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Demurrer And Demurrer To X-Complaints Darren Lee
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 14, 2011, Line 11. CROSS DEFENDANT GLORIA WONG Demurrer To Cross-Complaints Darren Lee. Overruled in its entirety with ten days to answer. The amended cross-complaint and judicially noticeable documents show that the cross-claims are based on facts that arose after the case number 460816 had settled. It cannot be determined as a matter of law that Ms. Lee lacks standing. Her joinder as a cross-complainant is permitted under Code of Civil Procedure 428.20. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THE PAYROLL RESOURCE GROUP, A CALIFORNIA VS. ELIZABETH ECKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Comply Wiht A Properly Served Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records, To Strike Objection
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 14, 2011, Line 7, PLAINTIFF THE PAYROLL RESOURCE GROUP's Motion To Comply With A Properly Served Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records, To Strike Objection. Granted in part and all requests for sanctions denied. All emails from October 1, 2010 through and including August 8, 2011 that are responsive to requests 1 and 2 must be produced no later than December 30, 2011 with an Evidence Code 1561 affidavit except that any documents claimed by the subpoenaed deponent and/or the defendants to contain trade secret information and/or attorney-client communications may be redacted or withheld. If any documents are redacted or withheld because they are asserted to contain trade secret information and/or are attorney-client communications, the subpoened deponent must provide a privilege log as to those documents. All objections other than trade secrets and attorney-client privilege lack merit. Neither side¿s positon lacked substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARCO LOBASCIO VS. CHRISITINE FLORENTINE AKA CHRISTINE DRAGHIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 14, 2011, Line 12. DEFENDANT JOCELYN SAMOY, CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the twelfth and fourteenth causes of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the thirteenth cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action. The Loan Proposal, which is the contract that forms the basis for the eleventh cause of action for breach of written contract, was not executed by the Chicago Title defendants. Plaintiff has not pled the underlying circumstances in which concealment alleged in the thirteenth cause of action constitutes actionable fraud, but is granted leave to do so if he can do so in good faith. The twelfth and fourteenth causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
